description: Redesign the Task Manager header into a compact single-row toolbar that keeps all controls (search, Active/All toggle, status filters, New Group, New Task) on one line without wrapping, maximizing vertical space for task visibility.
steps: Remove the 'Task Manager' text label and flex-wrap from the toolbar container to prevent multi-row wrapping
Resize the search box to ~180-220px fixed width, remove flex-1/max-w-xs behavior
Keep status filter pills compact as-is
Replace the group creation inline input with a single compact 'New Group' button that triggers the group creation form elsewhere or uses a simple prompt
Ensure everything fits within ~720px single row without wrapping
acceptance_criteria: The toolbar renders on a single row without wrapping at 720px+ width
Search box is always visible and functional
Active/All toggle is visible and functional
Status filter pills (Todo, In Progress, Waiting, Completed) are visible and functional
New Task button is visible and functional
Header height is reduced compared to the previous multi-row layout
npm run build succeeds without errors
verification: Run npm run build in /projects/sandbox/Clear-mind and confirm exit code 0
findings: Build succeeds. Replaced inline group-name input with prompt()-based button to save ~130px horizontal space. Reduced all padding, gaps, font sizes, and icon sizes. Removed flex-wrap and the 'Task Manager' heading. The toolbar now renders everything in a single row without wrapping. Pre-existing chunk size warning (844KB) remains.
